zh.lifeSummary: Thomas Bartholomew是Robert Bartholomew的兒子，於1542年出生在英國英格蘭素可福爾金頓。他於1567年7月4日與Agnes Abrooke在英國素可福爾金頓結婚。他們至少是4個兒子和3個女兒的父母。他去世於1570年3月在英國素可福爾金頓享年28歲，埋葬在英國素可福爾金頓。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: Thomas Bartholomew是Robert Bartholomew的儿子，于1542年出生在英国英格兰素可福爾金頓。他于1567年7月4日与Agnes Abrooke在英国素可福爾金頓结婚。他们至少是4个儿子和3个女儿的父母。他去世于1570年3月在英国素可福爾金頓享年28岁，埋葬在英国素可福爾金頓。

nameMeanings.name: Bartholomew
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: from the Middle English personal name Bertilmew, Bertelmy, a borrowing of the Old French form of the Biblical personal name Bartholomew (from Latin Bartholomaeus; Hebrew ‘son of Talmai’, said to mean ‘having many furrows’, i.e. rich in land). This was an extremely popular personal name in Christian Europe, with many vernacular derivatives. It derived its popularity from the apostle Saint Bartholomew (Matthew 10:3), who was, among other things, the patron saint of tanners, vintners, and butlers.
As an Irish name, it has been used as an Anglicized form of Mac Pharthaláin (see McFarlane ).

variants.name: McFarlane
meanings: Scottish and northern Irish: Anglicized form of Gaelic Mac Pharthaláin, a patronymic from the personal name Parthalán, which is most probably from Latin Bartholomaeus (see Bartholomew ). Parthalán, regularly shortened in speech to Parlán, has also been Anglicized as Bartlett . Compare McPartland .
History: McFarlane is a Scottish clan originally associated with Arrochar on Loch Long. — Robert McFarlane came to NY in 1835 and was editor of Scientific American magazine (1848–65).

variants.name: Bara
meanings: Polish: from a shortened vernacular form of the personal name Bartłomiej (see Bartholomew ).

Czech (Bára) and Slovak: from a pet form of the female personal name Barbora (Czech), Barbara (Slovak), or possibly of the male personal name Bartoloměj (Czech), Bartolomej (Slovak); see Bartholomew .
Hungarian: from a short form of the personal name Barabás (see Barabas ).

variants.name: Barak
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Jewish Pinhas, Yoram, Avital, Doron, Dov, Erez, Haim, Hanoch, Moshe, Ronit, Shai, Shimon.

Jewish (Israeli): from the Biblical Hebrew personal name Barak, meaning ‘lightning’. The surname mainly represents a Hebraicized form of several original eastern Ashkenazic surnames having similar consonants.

Jewish (eastern Ashkenazic): acronymic surname from the first letters of the Hebrew patronymic phrase Ben Rabi Kalonymos ‘son of rabbi Kalonymos’. The personal name Kalonymos means ‘beautiful name’ in Greek.

variants.name: Bac
meanings: Polish (also Bać): perhaps a variant of Bacz, from a pet form of the personal name Bartłomiej (see Bartholomew ), occuring in southeastern Poland. Aternatively, it may be a metonymic occupational name derived from baca ‘shepherd’. Compare Batz .
French: from bac ‘tub, trough’, probably applied as a metonymic occupational name or as a topographic name.
Slovenian: nickname from bac ‘lambkin’. Compare Batz .

variants.name: Baert
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Aime.
Flemish and Dutch:
from a short form of the personal name Bartholomaeus (see Bartholomew ), or from the female personal name Baerte or Bartha.

variants.name: Baczek
meanings: Polish:
derivative of the personal name Bacz, Bacza, a pet form of Bartłomiej (see Bartholomew ).
(Bączek): from bączek, a diminutive of bąk, which variously means ‘horse-fly’, ‘bittern’, or ‘eagle-owl’ (see Bak ).

variants.name: Arko
meanings: Slovenian: from a pet form of the personal names beginning with Ar-, most likely from Arnej, an old vernacular form of Bartolomej (see Bartholomew ).
Jewish (eastern Ashkenazic): from a pet form of the personal name Aron, Slavic form of the Biblical name Aaron .
West African (Ghana): from an Akan male personal name denoting a first-born boy.

variants.name: Barac
meanings: Croatian: from an old pet form of the personal name Bartolomej (see Bartholomew ) or of its vernacular variants Bartol or Bartul.
Croatian (Barać and Barač): from a derivative of the personal name Bare or Baro, short forms of Bartolomej (see Bartholomew ) or of its vernacular variants Bartol or Bartul.

Serbian (mainly Barać): topographic name derived from bara ‘swamp’ or a habitational name for someone from a place named Bare. It may also be of the same origin as the Croatian names above. Compare Barach .

variants.name: Bach
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Otto, Hans, Kurt, Fritz, Klaus, Horst, Johann, Alfons, Bernhard, Erna, Ernst.

German: topographic name for someone who lived by a stream, Middle High German bach ‘stream’. This surname is also found in some other European countries, most notably in Denmark and France (Alsace and Lorraine), but also in e.g. the Netherlands, Czechia (see also 5 below), and Croatia.
Jewish (Ashkenazic): artificial name from German Bach ‘stream, creek’.

variants.name: Baczewski
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Polish Zygmunt, Bogdan, Bronislaw, Danuta, Jerzy, Zbigniew, Zigmund, Zygfryd.
Polish:
habitational name for someone from a place called Bacze in Masovian Voivodeship.

nameMeanings.name: Thomas
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: New Testament name, borne by one of Christ's twelve apostles, referred to as ‘Thomas, called Didymus’ (John 11:16; 20:24). Didymos is the Greek word for ‘twin’, and the name is the Greek form of an Aramaic byname meaning ‘twin’. The given name has always been popular throughout Christendom, in part because St Thomas's doubts have made him seem a very human character.

historicEvents.title: Book of Common Prayer
historicEvents.description: Book of Common Prayer, a product of the English Reformation, was published in 1549 for assistance in the administration of the sacraments and other rites and ceremonies of the church according to the use of the Church of England. The book outlined morning, evening, and communion prayers and orders for baptism and marriage, thus making England a truly Protestant state.

historicEvents.title: Act of Uniformity
historicEvents.description: The Act of Uniformity was passed by the Parliament of England and required all people to go to church once a week. The consequence of not attending church was a fine of 12 pence, which was a considerable amount for a poor person.

historicEvents.title: State Lottery
historicEvents.description: A State Lottery was recorded in 1569. The tickets were sold at St. Paul's Cathedral in London.

en.lifeSummary: Thomas Bartholomew was born in 1542, in Folkington, Sussex, England, United Kingdom as the son of Robert Bartholomew. He married Agnes Abrooke on 4 July 1567, in Folkington, Sussex, England. They were the parents of at least 4 sons and 3 daughters. He died in March 1570, in Folkington, Sussex, England, at the age of 28, and was buried in Folkington, Sussex, England.

fr.lifeSummary: Thomas Bartholomew est né en 1542, à Folkington, Sussex, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, fils de Robert Bartholomew. Il a épousé Agnes Abrooke le 4 juillet 1567, à Folkington, Sussex, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 4 fils et 3 filles. Il est décédé en mars 1570, à Folkington, Sussex, Angleterre, à l’âge de 28 ans, et a été enterré à Folkington, Sussex, Angleterre.

de.lifeSummary: Thomas Bartholomew wurde 1542 in Folkington, Sussex, England, United Kingdom, als Sohn von Robert Bartholomew geboren. Er hat am 4. Juli 1567 in Folkington, Sussex, England Agnes Abrooke geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 4 Söhne und 3 Töchter. Er starb im März 1570 im Alter von 28 Jahren in Folkington, Sussex, England und wurde in Folkington, Sussex, England begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Thomas Bartholomew è nato il nel 1542 nel Folkington, Sussex, Inghilterra, Regno Unito come figlio di Robert Bartholomew. Lui sposato Agnes Abrooke il 4 luglio 1567, nel Folkington, Sussex, England. Erano genitori di almeno 4 figli e 3 figlie. Morì nel marzo 1570, nel Folkington, Sussex, England, all'età di 28 anni, e fu sepolto a Folkington, Sussex, England.

ja.lifeSummary: Thomas Bartholomewは1542年にイギリスイギリススセックスフォルキントンでRobert Bartholomewの息子として生まれた。1567年7月4日にイギリススセックスフォルキントンでAgnes Abrookeと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも4人の息子と3人の娘の親であった。彼は1570年3月にイギリススセックスフォルキントンで28歳で死去し，イギリススセックスフォルキントンにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Thomas Bartholomew(은)는 1542년 영국 잉글랜드 수세스 폴킨그턴에서 Robert Bartholomew의 아들로 태어났다. 그는 1567년 7월 4일 영국 수세스 폴킨그턴에서 Agnes Abrooke(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 네 명의 아들과 세 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1570년 3월 영국 수세스 폴킨그턴에서 28세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 수세스 폴킨그턴에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Thomas Bartholomew nasceu em 1542, em Folkington, Sussex, Inglaterra, United Kingdom filho de Robert Bartholomew. Ele casou-se com Agnes Abrooke em 4 de julho de 1567, em Folkington, Sussex, Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 4 filhos e 3 filhas. Ele faleceu em março de 1570, em Folkington, Sussex, Inglaterra, com 28 anos, e foi sepultado em Folkington, Sussex, Inglaterra.

ru.lifeSummary: Thomas Bartholomew родился в 1542 г. в местности фолкингтон, суссекс, Англия, Великобритания. Его отца звали Robert Bartholomew. Он женился на женщине по имени Agnes Abrooke 4 июля 1567 г. в местности фолкингтон, суссекс, Англия. У них было по крайней мере 4 сына и 3 дочери. Он умер марта 1570 г. в местности фолкингтон, суссекс, Англия в возрасте 28 лет и был похоронен в местности фолкингтон, суссекс, Англия.

es.lifeSummary: Thomas Bartholomew nació en 1542, en Folkington, Sussex, Inglaterra, Reino Unido como hijo de Robert Bartholomew. Se casó con Agnes Abrooke el 4 de julio de 1567, en Folkington, Sussex, Inglaterra. Fueron padres de por lo menos 4 hijos y 3 hijas. Murió en marzo de 1570, en Folkington, Sussex, Inglaterra, a la edad de 28 años, y fue sepultado en Folkington, Sussex, Inglaterra.
